note_data.title: People in organisations
keyPoints: Business activity aims to meet customer needs by using resources to make goods and services that customers will buy
Products can be either for final consumers or capital and intermediate goods for business customers
Four factors of production are needed: Labour (human effort), Capital (equipment and machinery), Land (location to operate from), and Enterprise (risk taking and decision making by entrepreneur)
Opportunity cost is an important concept in business activity and business decisions
When a decision is made to purchase one item, other items have to be 'given up' as resources are scarce
The concept of opportunity cost applies to all business resource allocation decisions

definitions.definition: Using resources to make goods and services that customers will buy to meet their needs
Human effort to produce goods or provide services to customers
Equipment and machinery used in business production
A location to operate the business from
Risk taking and decision making undertaken by an entrepreneur
What has to be 'given up' when making a business decision as resources are scarce

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Break down into parts and explain the impact/consequences
Give advantages AND disadvantages, then make a justified conclusion

modelAnswer.question: Explain two benefits to Packages Ltd of using democratic leadership style. [6]
modelAnswer.answer: One benefit is improved employee motivation because workers feel valued when their opinions are consulted in decision-making, leading to higher productivity. Another benefit is better quality decisions because the manager receives input from employees who have detailed knowledge of day-to-day operations, reducing the risk of costly mistakes.
markBreakdown: Mark 2: Explanation - consultation makes workers feel valued
Mark 3: Development - leading to higher productivity
Mark 5: Explanation - employee input and knowledge

commonMistakes: Confusing consumer goods with capital goods and their different target markets
Not understanding that all four factors of production are essential for business activity
Failing to recognize that opportunity cost applies to all resource allocation decisions, not just major purchases
examinerTips: Remember that business activity involves transforming resources (inputs) into products (outputs) for customers
Always consider opportunity cost when analyzing business decisions about resource allocation
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Maslow's hierarchy of needs pyramid showing five levels from bottom: physiological, safety/security, social/belonging, esteem/recognition, self-actualisation. Essential for explaining how different rewards motivate different employees.
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q4 (6 marks) — they asked students to analyze the impact of poor communication on employee motivation in a manufacturing company
N/D 2022 P1 Q8 (4 marks) tested knowledge of span of control by asking candidates to explain why a restaurant manager should have a narrow span of control
M/J 2022 P2 Q3 (8 marks) required students to evaluate different leadership styles for managing a team of software developers
N/D 2023 P1 Q12 (3 marks) asked candidates to identify and explain methods of employee training suitable for a retail chain
M/J 2021 P2 Q5 (10 marks) tested organizational structure by asking students to recommend the best structure for a growing e-commerce business

workedExample.question: ABC Electronics is experiencing high employee turnover. The CEO uses an autocratic leadership style and there is poor upward communication. Evaluate two methods the company could use to improve employee retention. (8 marks)
steps: Step 1: Identify the problems - autocratic leadership and poor upward communication causing turnover
Step 2: Choose two relevant methods - democratic leadership style and improved communication systems
Step 3: Explain each method with business context and analyze advantages/disadvantages
Step 4: Make a judgment on which method would be most effective for ABC Electronics
workedExample.answer: Method 1: Implement democratic leadership style. This involves the CEO consulting employees in decision-making and delegating authority. Advantages include increased employee motivation as workers feel valued, and better decisions through employee input. However, it may slow down decision-making. Method 2: Establish formal upward communication channels like suggestion boxes and regular team meetings. This allows employee feedback to reach management, improving job satisfaction. Disadvantages include time costs and potential for negative feedback. Recommendation: Democratic leadership would be most effective as it directly addresses the autocratic style causing dissatisfaction, leading to better retention.
workedExample.markScheme: 2 marks for each method identified, 2 marks for explanation of each method, 1 mark for advantages/disadvantages of each, 1 mark for evaluation/judgment

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused span of control with chain of command' — always define span of control as the number of subordinates directly managed by one person
Examiner Report 2022: 'Students often described motivation theories without linking them to the business context' — always apply Maslow's hierarchy or Herzberg's theory to the specific scenario given

Examiner Report 2023: 'Weak answers on leadership styles failed to explain when each style is appropriate' — always mention that autocratic suits crisis situations, democratic suits skilled workers, and laissez-faire suits creative teams
note_data.keyDiagram: Students must draw an organizational chart showing hierarchical structure with CEO at top, followed by functional managers (Marketing, Finance, Operations, HR), then department supervisors, and finally operational staff. Include clear lines showing chain of command, label each level, and indicate span of control by showing how many subordinates each manager supervises. Add arrows to show formal communication flow up and down the hierarchy.
note_data.quickRecap: Business activity uses four factors of production (labour, capital, land, enterprise) to create goods and services for customers. Products can target final consumers or other businesses. Opportunity cost is crucial in business decisions as choosing one option means giving up alternatives due to scarce resources.
